_comment: rootfs_bytes_max is the BLOCKING per-board content ceiling (Task 20). measure-size.sh sizes rootfs CONTENT via `du --apparent-size -sb` (real file bytes, NOT the frozen 4096 MB A/B partition — G4/E5) and exits non-zero when measured > rootfs_bytes_max. 1500000000 (1.5 GB) is a conservative post-slim ceiling: a minimal Debian bookworm arm64 rootfs with the slimmed shared.list (~35 pkgs, T17) + GStreamer + modem stack measures ~0.8-1.2 GB apparent, leaving headroom. This is a placeholder ceiling that the first real arm64 build refines downward (record the measured value, set the ceiling just above it). Keys beginning with '_' are metadata, not board entries.

_baked_binaries._comment: Non-apt binaries fetched + staged into the rootfs at build time (postinst-lib.sh), recorded here so the per-board rootfs_bytes_max headroom stays auditable. bytes = staged binary size measured 2026-07-02; all comfortably under the 1.5 GB ceiling. Metadata only — NOT a board entry (measure-size.sh ignores '_'-prefixed keys).

mediamtx.note: RTMP ingest gateway (Todo 14). Per-arch static Go binary; ~52 MiB (amd64) / ~59 MiB (arm64). The plan estimated ~30 MB; the real measured delta is recorded here.
